summary_one_line: Input must encode a complete URL scheme plus a parseable HTTP-like server response so the request actually receives bytes and bytecount+headerbytecount != 0.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must contain (a) a recognized URL-scheme literal at the front (winners carry 'pop3+-' at offsets 0x06-0x0b, an extended pop3 form curl's URL parser accepts) followed by a host:port spec like '127.0.0.1:9021', AND (b) a synthetic server-response payload at offsets 0x20-0x2b that begins with the literal 'HTTP/' so curl's response parser accepts framing bytes and increments data->req.bytecount/headerbytecount, making the sum at line 1866 non-zero (the FALSE side).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner seeds share the literal byte sequence 70 6f 70 33 2b 2d ('pop3+-') at offsets 0x06-0x0b and 47 48 54 54 50 2f ('GHTTP/') at offsets 0x24-0x29, which drives the request through the read/write data path: HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE shows readwrite_upload, Curl_fillreadbuffer, Curl_done_sending, and Curl_http2_done_sending all fire on W with zero L hits, and DIVERGENT BRANCHES confirms W takes the FALSE side ten times at L1866 (T=0 F=10), meaning bytecount+headerbytecount evaluated > 0.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are short and unstructured (32-38 bytes for four of five) with no concentrated bytes at 0x06-0x0b (variants 31 5a 2b 25 41, no 'pop3+-' literal) and no 'HTTP/' literal anywhere in 0x24-0x29 (L bytes there are 6f/41/4b/dc — random havoc residue), so the URL never resolves to a valid connection and no response bytes are read; DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows L takes TRUE every time at L1866 (T=10 F=0) because bytecount+headerbytecount stays at zero, and HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE shows zero L invocations of Curl_fillreadbuffer and readwrite_upload.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: curl's URL parser performs literal string CMPs against scheme names ('pop3', '+', '-' suffix characters) and its response parser performs a literal CMP against 'HTTP/' at the start of an incoming buffer; cmplog's CmpLogObserver captures both pairs of operands and I2SRandReplace substitutes them into the input at matching offsets, planting 'pop3+-' at 0x06 and 'HTTP/' at 0x24 in one mutation rather than blindly stumbling onto 11 consecutive ASCII bytes. value_profile_cmplog inherits the same I2SRandReplace mutator on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback, which is why both vpc>vp and cmplog>naive are decisive on the I2S axis.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the bytecount+headerbytecount==0 conjunct at transfer.c:1866 column 6 inside Curl_retry_request; the blocked side is FALSE.
Globally blocked side is FALSE, meaning the winner needs to take the FALSE branch (sum is non-zero).
Cmplog and value_profile_cmplog resolve all ten trials; naive and value_profile resolve zero of ten.
Both decisive pairs differ on the I2S technique only (cmplog vs naive, value_profile_cmplog vs value_profile).
The second decisive pair (vpc>vp) is also a single-axis I2S difference.
At L1866 the winner side takes the false branch ten times and the loser takes the true branch ten times — a clean directional flip on this exact branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1866  T=0 F=10  T=10 F=0  if((data->req.bytecount + data->req.headerbytecount == 0) &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The read/upload data-path functions fire only on the winner side, confirming the winner actually received/transferred bytes.
Curl_fillreadbuffer (the function that drains response bytes into the request buffer) is W-only.
All ten winner seeds share the literal bytes 'pop3+-' at offsets 0x06-0x0b (concentrated single-value sets per offset).
Offset 0x0a is consistently '+' on W and scattered random bytes on L — characteristic of an I2S-substituted scheme separator.
All ten winner seeds carry the literal bytes 'GHTTP' at offsets 0x24-0x28 (single-value W sets, scattered L sets).
Winner seed 1 contains the full 'HTTP/' literal at bytes 0x24-0x29 followed by space and 'mK@Sn' header text.
Winner seed 2 contains the 'pop3+-' scheme literal at offsets 0x06-0x0b and a host:port string '127.0.0.1' starting at 0x0d.
Loser seeds are short (32-38 bytes) and carry no 'HTTP/' or 'pop3+-' literal — example loser seed 1 ends at 32 bytes with random havoc bytes.
Cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of naive's havoc+token stack — the mutator that scans input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other.
Value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and relies on CMP_MAP gradient feedback only, which explains why it loses to value_profile_cmplog on this branch.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 422 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 006459fd40731a4e`. The chain has 21 ancestor levels with two I2S-floor dash rows at depth 19 and 20 (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only metadata). Per the cmplog MECHANISM CONTEXT note, dash rows are exclusive to cmplog/vpc in the current LibAFL build (zero occurrences in 6000 naive/vp samples) and indicate at least one unwrapped I2SRandReplace substitution somewhere in the ancestor chain — a lower-bound corroboration that this winning seed inherits from an I2S find, consistent with the concentrated 'pop3+-' and 'HTTP/' literals planted at fixed offsets in all winner seeds.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a hand-crafted input that omits the literal 'HTTP/' at offsets 0x24-0x29 and uses an arbitrary 4-byte placeholder there still drives bytecount+headerbytecount above zero (e.g. because some other code path on the harness increments those counters independently of the response parser), then 'HTTP/' literal substitution is not the required gate and the I2S-substitution mechanism story is wrong.

weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row floor confirms an I2SRandReplace contribution somewhere in the seed's 21-level ancestor chain, but the dash rows sit at depths 19-20 (near the root); we cannot mechanically prove that the specific 'HTTP/' bytes at 0x24-0x29 in the leaf came from I2S rather than from lucky havoc on an I2S-seeded ancestor, nor can we distinguish whether the 'pop3+-' scheme literal or the 'HTTP/' response literal is the dominant I2S target.
